categories.label: Image animation vs video generation
categories.description: image_animation = drive/edit a still via lipsync, warp, or 3DMM paste (source pixels largely preserved). video_generation = synthesize new frame pixels with a generative model (diffusion/flow), usually still image-conditioned — not text-to-video from scratch. Sonic is the latter (SVD img2vid).

categories.label: Visual fidelity / natural vs uncanny
categories.description: Manual score after watching the same clip set. Identity, skin, teeth, blinks, head motion, composite seams, and uncanny valley feel.

categories.label: Concurrency / scalability
categories.description: Effective concurrent sessions per GPU = 0.85/busy_ratio (fractional), capped by VRAM. Values below 1 mean the model is slower than realtime.

categories.label: Latency & conversation speed
categories.description: Time to first spoken frame, chunk gen time, realtime factor. Isolated from LiveKit/LLM where possible.

categories.label: Hosting & server flexibility
categories.description: Curated ops score (not UI-editable): self-host, Windows, deps fragility, license, day-2 restart pain. High = drop-in; low = heavy/fragile/restricted.

categories.label: Cost
categories.description: GPU $/session-hour = $0.44 ÷ effective sessions/GPU. Effective sessions = 0.85/busy_ratio (fractional; <1 if slower than realtime), also capped by VRAM. Not floored to 1.

models.name: Wav2Lip
models.notes: Classic lip-sync on face crop; no idle motion (static between utterances). Must paste into locked face rect. Shoulders/body stay static.

models.name: MuseTalk
models.notes: Latent lipsync on a face crop, blended back onto the still. Use locked composite / full-frame blend.

models.name: SadTalker
models.notes: Audio→3DMM coeffs→neural face render, then paste onto still (full/still mode). Offline fidelity baseline, not conversation-realtime.

models.name: Sonic
models.notes: CVPR 2025 audio-driven portrait via Stable Video Diffusion (img2vid) — generative video frames conditioned on a still + audio, not lipsync paste. High fidelity, not sub-3s. CC BY-NC-SA (non-commercial).

models.name: LivePortrait
models.notes: FasterLivePortrait ONNX warp + JoyVASA audio→motion. Identity-stable pasteback onto still. MIT.

models.name: LiveTalk
models.notes: Distilled causal Wan 1.3B (4-step). FlashHead-class realtime candidate. Official Linux+flash-attn; Windows SDPA best-effort. Public code is clip infer (not WebRTC stream).

models.name: EchoMimicV3-Flash
models.notes: 1.3B Wan-Fun audio-driven animation. Stronger motion/look than Lite FlashHead; utterance clips, not infinite stream. Apache-2.0.

models.name: Ditto
models.notes: Motion-space diffusion synthesizing talking-head frames from a still. Open path is clip/PyTorch; paper realtime needs TensorRT.

models.name: SoulX-FlashHead Lite
models.notes: Continuous generative talking-head stream (not clip-offline). Full-image mode = static bg + locked face overlay. Best speed/quality tradeoff for chat.

models.name: SoulX-FlashHead Pro
models.notes: Same continuous stream stack as Lite; Wan VAE + Model_Pro. Higher detail, not conversation-realtime on one 4090. Setup: .\setup_flashhead_pro.ps1

details.name: Wav2Lip
details.catalog_notes: Classic lip-sync on face crop; no idle motion (static between utterances). Must paste into locked face rect. Shoulders/body stay static.
details.cost_notes: $/sess-hr = $0.44/hr ÷ 5.6 eff. sessions (0.85/busy_ratio, capped by VRAM). <1 session means slower-than-realtime.
details.fidelity_notes: Mouth region poorly aligned with identity; no idle motion between utterances. Fast but below visual quality bar.
details.hosting_notes: Small classic stack; face crop + composite. Easy Windows self-host.
details.verdict: Fast and cheap, but visual quality is far below product bar for this portrait.
pros: Very high realtime factor and concurrency on 4090
Small stack; easy Windows self-host
Lowest GPU cost in the matrix
cons: Mouth region poorly matches identity; uncanny and misaligned
No idle motion (static between utterances)
Requires locked face composite; shoulders stay frozen

details.name: MuseTalk
details.catalog_notes: Latent lipsync on a face crop, blended back onto the still. Use locked composite / full-frame blend.
details.cost_notes: $/sess-hr = $0.44/hr ÷ 0.807 eff. sessions (0.85/busy_ratio, capped by VRAM). <1 session means slower-than-realtime.
details.fidelity_notes: Mouth region quality below product bar despite near-realtime speed.
details.hosting_notes: Separate venv; mmpose/mmcv fragile on Windows; VRAM-sensitive.
details.verdict: Near-realtime but mouth quality and Windows ops hold it back.
pros: Roughly realtime on this harness
Latent lipsync with composite back onto the still
Moderate cost vs slower generative peers
cons: Mouth appearance scored poorly in manual review
Separate venv; mmpose/mmcv fragile on Windows
VRAM-sensitive; no continuous idle stream

details.name: SadTalker
details.catalog_notes: Audio→3DMM coeffs→neural face render, then paste onto still (full/still mode). Offline fidelity baseline, not conversation-realtime.
details.cost_notes: $/sess-hr = $0.44/hr ÷ 0.33 eff. sessions (0.85/busy_ratio, capped by VRAM). <1 session means slower-than-realtime.
details.fidelity_notes: Blurry, low-resolution output; offline clip path only — too slow for conversation.
details.hosting_notes: Own venv + basicsr/gfpgan/numpy patches; offline clip path only.
details.verdict: Useful as an offline baseline only — too blurry and slow for chat.
pros: Established 3DMM portrait pipeline
Lip sync acceptable relative to its resolution
Uncanny score better than warp/lipsync failures in this set
cons: Blurry / low-resolution output
Offline clip path; not conversation-realtime
Own venv and dependency patches on Windows

details.name: Sonic
details.catalog_notes: CVPR 2025 audio-driven portrait via Stable Video Diffusion (img2vid) — generative video frames conditioned on a still + audio, not lipsync paste. High fidelity, not sub-3s. CC BY-NC-SA (non-commercial).
details.cost_notes: $/sess-hr = $0.44/hr ÷ 0.056 eff. sessions (0.85/busy_ratio, capped by VRAM). <1 session means slower-than-realtime.
details.fidelity_notes: Highest visual fidelity in the set; utterance render times remain minutes-class on a 4090.
details.hosting_notes: Huge SVD stack; CC BY-NC-SA; minutes/utt; wants 24–32GB GPU.
details.verdict: Highest visual fidelity in the set; far too slow and license-restricted for chat.
pros: Top manual fidelity / identity / lip scores
Generative SVD img2vid quality stands out
Strong composite when used with locked framing
cons: Minutes-class latency per utterance (~0.07× realtime)
CC BY-NC-SA (non-commercial)
Huge stack and VRAM appetite; poor concurrency and $/sess-hr

details.name: LivePortrait
details.catalog_notes: FasterLivePortrait ONNX warp + JoyVASA audio→motion. Identity-stable pasteback onto still. MIT.
details.cost_notes: $/sess-hr = $0.44/hr ÷ 0.114 eff. sessions (0.85/busy_ratio, capped by VRAM). <1 session means slower-than-realtime.
details.fidelity_notes: Multiple ONNX/JoyVASA configurations reviewed; output remained unusable for the target portrait and render times stayed far below realtime.
details.hosting_notes: FasterLivePortrait ONNX + JoyVASA + HuBERT; MIT; onnxruntime CUDA pins; multi-GB downloads.
details.verdict: Did not meet visual or latency bar in this Windows ONNX + JoyVASA setup.
pros: MIT license; documented FasterLivePortrait path
Hosting/ops score middling (installable, not impossible)
cons: Manual fidelity scored at floor after multiple configs
Extremely slow render in this harness
Warp/composite result looked unusable for the target portrait

details.name: LiveTalk
details.catalog_notes: Distilled causal Wan 1.3B (4-step). FlashHead-class realtime candidate. Official Linux+flash-attn; Windows SDPA best-effort. Public code is clip infer (not WebRTC stream).
details.automated_notes: Paper claims ~25 FPS on Linux+flash-attn; Windows SDPA path far slower. Clip infer only — no continuous idle stream.
details.fidelity_notes: Occasional strong frames, but drift and mushy identity between clips; no usable idle; too slow for dialogue.
details.hosting_notes: Linux+flash-attn oriented; heavy Wan stack; fragile Windows install; ~20GB VRAM.
details.verdict: Paper realtime does not translate to this Windows path; not chat-ready here.
pros: Interesting distilled Wan architecture on paper
Apache-2.0
cons: Windows SDPA path far slower than Linux+flash-attn claims
Clip infer only — no continuous idle
Identity drift between clips; heavy VRAM and fragile install

details.name: EchoMimicV3-Flash
details.catalog_notes: 1.3B Wan-Fun audio-driven animation. Stronger motion/look than Lite FlashHead; utterance clips, not infinite stream. Apache-2.0.
details.automated_notes: EchoMimicV3-Flash 8-step on 4090 ~0.8 FPS class; utterance clips only. Warm load alone is minutes.
details.fidelity_notes: Motion can look rich in isolation, but turn latency and stutter kill conversation; no continuous idle.
details.hosting_notes: Large HF pull; long cold start; flash-pro path still offline-class for chat.
details.verdict: Offline-class clip model; motion quality cannot save conversation latency.
pros: Rich motion in isolated clips
Apache-2.0; documented flash-pro path
cons: ~0.01× realtime; warm load alone is minutes
No continuous idle stream
Worst GPU $/session-hour in this matrix

details.name: Ditto
details.catalog_notes: Motion-space diffusion synthesizing talking-head frames from a still. Open path is clip/PyTorch; paper realtime needs TensorRT.
details.automated_notes: PyTorch/Windows path; paper realtime needs TensorRT which did not land cleanly here.
details.fidelity_notes: Still frames can look fine; lips and timing fall apart under dialogue; no live idle stream.
details.hosting_notes: TRT path painful on Windows; open PyTorch clip path too slow for product chat.
details.verdict: Open PyTorch path is too slow; TensorRT realtime did not land cleanly on Windows.
pros: Still frames can look fine in isolation
Composite path available for full-image framing
cons: Dialogue timing and lips fall apart under load
No live idle stream on the open path
TRT dependency for claimed realtime; fragile Windows ops

details.name: SoulX-FlashHead Lite
details.catalog_notes: Continuous generative talking-head stream (not clip-offline). Full-image mode = static bg + locked face overlay. Best speed/quality tradeoff for chat.
details.automated_notes: Measured locally; LLM+TTS dominate TTFW. Model-only gen is gen_ms / chunk_seconds.
details.cost_notes: $/sess-hr = $0.44/hr ÷ 1.728 eff. sessions (0.85/busy_ratio, capped by VRAM). <1 session means slower-than-realtime.
details.fidelity_notes: Solid continuous idle and speech. Occasional uncanny moments; overall strong for conversational use.
details.hosting_notes: Vendored + HF weights; Windows needs xfuser stub / no triton.
details.verdict: Best overall for conversational talking-head on a single RTX 4090 in this bench.
pros: Continuous idle + speech in one stream (~2× realtime on 4090)
Strong lip sync and stable identity in full-image composite
Lowest GPU $/session-hour among generative peers tested
Self-hostable on Windows with a manageable weight download
cons: Occasional uncanny moments in idle/expression
Windows needs compatibility stubs (no Triton / xfuser)
Composite still required for full-frame portrait lock

details.name: SoulX-FlashHead Pro
details.catalog_notes: Same continuous stream stack as Lite; Wan VAE + Model_Pro. Higher detail, not conversation-realtime on one 4090. Setup: .\setup_flashhead_pro.ps1
details.automated_notes: Single-4090 Pro path; idle and speech both far below realtime. Must buffer full replies to avoid freezes.
details.fidelity_notes: Detail spikes briefly then collapses into freezes; idle unusable; composite seams obvious under load.
details.hosting_notes: Same Lite stack but Model_Pro + Wan VAE; VRAM and restart cost higher; not chat-viable on one 4090.
details.verdict: Not viable for live chat on one 4090; detail does not offset freezes and cost.
pros: Same API surface as Lite; easy A/B
Brief moments of higher facial detail when a chunk completes
cons: ~0.2× realtime (~5.5 s gen per ~1.1 s video) — constant underruns
Idle generative motion unusable without multi-second freezes
Higher VRAM and heavier Wan VAE path than Lite
Must buffer full replies to play smoothly, killing conversational latency

hardware_note: Primary harness: RTX 4090-class GPU, Windows, torch.compile off unless noted.
